signals.headline: Key details:** Trump's nomination of former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh to succeed Jerome Powell (expected mid-May) has div
signals.raw: Key details:** Trump's nomination of former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh to succeed Jerome Powell (expected mid-May) has divided analysts, with some expecting hawkish rate hikes due to **annual core inflation at 3.9%** (pre-oil shock, pushing 3-month annualized core PCE to 4.56%, double the 2% target) and oil above $100/barrel from Middle East conflict[1]. signals.headline: **US-Iran ceasefire status**: Temporary two-week truce entered over a week ago after 40 days of hostilities; first round
signals.raw: **US-Iran ceasefire status**: Temporary two-week truce entered over a week ago after 40 days of hostilities; first round of talks ended without deal on nuclear issues or Strait of Hormuz control (Iran maintains de facto grip, choking 20% of world oil).[1][3]
matched_markets: Will the US x Iran ceasefire be extended by April 21, 2026?
Will 80 ships transit the Strait of Hormuz on any day by April 30?

signals.headline: **Recent developments**: US blockade of Iranian ports fully implemented; Chinese/Iranian tankers reportedly broke Hormuz
signals.raw: **Recent developments**: US blockade of Iranian ports fully implemented; Chinese/Iranian tankers reportedly broke Hormuz blockade; Iran avoids direct US attacks but targets Gulf allies like Bahrain with drones; ceasefire seen as fragile with violations from start (e.g., Hormuz not opened).[2][3][4]
matched_markets: Will the US x Iran ceasefire be extended by April 21, 2026?

signals.headline: **Impact on prediction markets**: No new confirmations/announcements today resolving ceasefire—ongoing tensions (blockad
signals.raw: **Impact on prediction markets**: No new confirmations/announcements today resolving ceasefire—ongoing tensions (blockades, strikes in Lebanon) signal high escalation risk, unlikely to move markets toward de-escalation without Hormuz/oil resolution.[1][3][4]
